adjudication_raw:
Review: Receipt OCR evaluation — startup VLM vs. commercial baseline.

Verdict: empirical_test_agreed

Two configuration issues are present: (1) the vendor's receipt-specific enterprise parser was not used — a general-document configuration was used instead; (2) 30% of test receipts are bilingual English-French but the vendor ran English-only while the startup was bilingual fine-tuned. Both issues disadvantage the vendor in ways unrelated to intrinsic model quality. Whether the 8.4 pp gap survives a fair comparison is an open empirical question. The startup's specialization suggests a genuine advantage may persist, but the magnitude under controlled conditions is unknown.

Empirical test required: run vendor with receipt-specific parser and bilingual settings on the same evaluation set. If gap ≥ 5 pp persists, startup's superiority claim holds.

empirical_test.measure: Exact-field accuracy on 8,400-receipt evaluation set with reconfigured vendor
empirical_test.success_criterion: Startup leads by ≥ 5 pp with vendor using receipt-specific parser and bilingual settings
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Gap below 5 pp under fair vendor configuration
